pattern: Tracking, for every prefix length, how many ways reach it while expecting each of the two target XOR values next reduces a partition-counting problem to a running tally indexed by prefix XOR.
hints: Work with prefix XOR values: the XOR of any contiguous block equals the XOR of the whole prefix up to its end, with the XOR of the whole prefix up to its start canceled out.

Define, for every prefix length, two running counts: the number of ways to have validly partitioned everything before it while the next block still owes target1, and the number of ways while it still owes target2; a new block closes out one of these counts and opens the other.

Rather than trying every possible block start for every possible end, group the running counts by their prefix XOR value in a lookup table, so each new position can look up exactly how many valid ways lead into it in constant time; sum both running counts at the very end, modulo 1e9 + 7.

edge_inputs.description: the reference example with exactly one valid alternating partition
the reference example with three valid alternating partitions
the reference example where the only possible single-block partition does not match target1
a single element whose value equals target1, the only valid partition is one block
an array of all zeros against a target1 of zero, with many equally-valid single-element blocks
values at the maximum allowed magnitude
no split of the array can ever reach target1 or target2, so the answer is zero
many repeated small values that XOR to zero in pairs, creating several overlapping ways to alternate

stress.description: random small values at a large length to build up many overlapping partitions and exercise the modulus
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 3) for _ in range(n)]
    target1 = rng.randint(0, 3)
    target2 = rng.randint(0, 3)
    while target2 == target1:
        target2 = rng.randint(0, 3)
    return [nums, target1, target2]

stress.description: random values across the full range at the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    target1 = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    target2 = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    while target2 == target1:
        target2 = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [nums, target1, target2]
